structure.details: Valid frontmatter with name and description. Missing not_for boundaries. Heading hierarchy clean but no environment requirements declared.

issues.item: No scope boundaries or not_for field declared

trigger.details: Vague description: 'optimizes SQL queries'. No specific keywords, no not_for, no locale awareness. Would false-trigger on any database-related query.

issues.item: No not_for or negative boundary statements
Description too broad — would overlap with general database skills

findings.description: User-provided SQL query interpolated directly into shell command: psql -c "${user_query}"
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or escape user input before shell interpolation

findings.description: Requests Bash tool access without documenting why shell access is needed
findings.recommendation: Document the purpose of shell access in environment requirements

security.details: CRITICAL: Command injection vulnerability. User SQL input passed directly to psql shell command without sanitization.

functional.details: Basic query optimization flow defined but lacks edge case handling, no output format specified, error handling minimal.

issues.item: No handling for invalid SQL input
No output format defined — optimization suggestions vary in structure
No error handling for database connection failures

test_cases: Simple SELECT optimization → should suggest index
Complex JOIN query → should analyze query plan

comparative.details: Moderate improvement for complex queries where tool integration helps, but minimal advantage for simple optimizations that LLMs handle well.

uniqueness.details: Good uniqueness. PostgreSQL-specific optimization with potential tool integration (EXPLAIN ANALYZE). No known overlapping skills in registry.

recommendations: CRITICAL: Fix command injection vulnerability in SQL execution (D3)
Add not_for boundaries to description to reduce false triggers (D2)
Define output format for optimization suggestions (D4)
Add error handling for database connection failures (D4)
